_meta.author: scorecard-refine-local cron
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize Houston TX local batch: HPD Chief Diaz + 4 city council (Castillo D-H, Castex-Tatum D-K, Huffman R-G, Alcorn D-AL5). Local tier, social-media/minutes protocol. Most culture-war cells genuinely NO EVIDENCE for municipal officials -> null. Immigration scored consistently: consistent ICE-limiters (Castillo, Diaz) FALSE on opposing-sanctuary; consistent anti-limiter (Huffman) TRUE; flip-floppers under state-funding duress (Castex-Tatum, Alcorn) null.

j-noe-diaz-jr.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. Appointed nonpartisan administrator (Chief of Police, HPD), confirmed by City Council Aug 2 2024; sitting Chief through 2026 (named in official capacity in AG Paxton's Apr 2026 SB4 suit). No votes apply; most social-policy cells genuinely NO EVIDENCE (correct for an appointed chief). public_justice q0 TRUE: affirmative pro-officer advocacy -- championed the May 2025 $832M HPOU contract ('generational and life-changing'), aggressive recruitment ('Be the Blue'), credited with restoring 264k+ suspended cases. border q2 FALSE: as Chief announced (Mar 2026) a policy giving ICE only a 30-min window to respond to administrative warrants before release, and is a named defendant in Paxton's anti-sanctuary suit -- documented record constrains, not maximizes, ICE cooperation. No evidence on life/marriage/family/Christian liberty/economic/election/self-defense.

0.note: Affirmative pro-law-enforcement advocacy: championed the $832M HPOU officer-pay contract as 'generational and life-changing,' ran aggressive recruitment, restored 264k+ suspended cases.

2.note: Announced (Mar 2026) a policy giving ICE only a 30-min window for administrative warrants before releasing the person; named defendant in AG Paxton's Apr 2026 SB4 sanctuary-city suit. Documented record limits ICE cooperation.

mario-castillo.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. Seat verified: District H, took office Jan 2 2024, term to Jan 2028 (houstontx.gov, Ballotpedia). Only openly LGBTQ+ council member; former Board Chair of the Houston LGBTQ+ Political Caucus. marriage q0 FALSE (does not affirm exclusive man/woman marriage -- openly LGBTQ+, Caucus chair) and q4 FALSE (affirmatively promotes LGBTQ+ identity in public policy: secured $75k to continue the city's LGBTQ+ Economic Empowerment Coordinator, presents Pride proclamations, endorsed by LGBTQ Victory Fund). border q1 & q2 FALSE: pro-immigrant -- defended council restrictions barring HPD from holding illegal aliens for ICE and paused his district's HPD overtime funds in protest of HPD-ICE coordination (Nov 2025). Has HPOU endorsement + HPD Training Advisory Board seat but recently withheld police OT funds (ICE-driven), so public_justice left null (no clean affirmative anti-defund advocacy). No evidence on life/schools/Christian liberty/election/self-defense; flat-tax vote and budget support did not amount to a balanced-budget position (null).

0.note: Openly LGBTQ+; former Board Chair of the Houston LGBTQ+ Political Caucus; endorsed by LGBTQ Victory Fund -- does not affirm exclusive man/woman marriage.

4.note: Affirmatively promotes LGBTQ+ identity in public policy: secured $75k to continue the city's LGBTQ+ Economic Empowerment Coordinator; presents Pride Month proclamations.

1.note: Pro-immigrant; defended restrictions barring HPD from holding illegal foreign nationals for ICE -- opposes mandatory deportation cooperation.

2.note: Paused his district's HPD overtime funds protesting HPD-ICE coordination; defended council limits on ICE cooperation -- pro-sanctuary posture.

martha-castex-tatum.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. Seat verified: District K, took office May 2018, second/final term to Jan 2028 (term-limited); Mayor Pro Tem under Mayor Whitmire (Ballotpedia, houstontx.gov). public_justice q1 FALSE: co-signed the Aug 2020 council police-reform letter advocating cite-and-release for minor offenses (soft-on-crime leaning), though explicitly 'not anti-police' and backed HPD funding increases -- so q0 left null (reform-leaning, not affirmative pro-police advocacy). Immigration left null: she initially supported the Apr 2026 ICE-limiting ordinance, then voted (13-4) to roll it back under threat of ~$110M lost state grants ('this may feel like surrender... it's real stewardship') -- a coerced flip, genuinely mixed. No evidence on life/marriage substance (presided over ceremonial Pride proclamations as Mayor Pro Tem but no personal position), schools, Christian liberty, election, economic-balanced-budget, or self-defense.

1.note: Co-signed Aug 2020 council letter pushing cite-and-release for minor offenses -- a soft-on-crime policy. (Explicitly 'not anti-police,' backed funding increases, so not scored as defunding.)

mary-nan-huffman.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. Seat verified: District G, sworn Feb 2022, re-elected 2024, term to Jan 2028 (houstontx.gov, Ballotpedia). Former general counsel for the Houston Police Officers' Union; 10-yr felony prosecutor (Montgomery Co. ICAC/child-exploitation). STRONG, consistent law-and-order record: public_justice q0 TRUE (HPOU + multiple police-association endorsements; 'support first responders, more academy classes'), q1 TRUE (as HPOU counsel publicly opposed the Harris Co. misdemeanor bail-reform settlement -- 'caters to criminals'; said a repeat offender in a triple homicide 'should have been behind bars'), q2 TRUE (career felony prosecutor; proportional-punishment advocacy). border q2 TRUE: voted AGAINST the Apr 8 2026 ordinance limiting HPD-ICE coordination (consistent anti-sanctuary). DEVIATION FLAG: a Republican-endorsed law-and-order official with a strong policing brand but ZERO documented record on life, marriage/LGBTQ, schools, Christian liberty, election integrity, or guns -- all left null, NOT inferred from party. Fiscal-conservative tax-rate dissent noted but not a balanced-budget position (null).

0.note: Former HPOU general counsel; endorsed by HPOU and multiple police associations; campaign platform 'support first responders... more academy classes.' Affirmative pro-police advocacy.

1.note: As HPOU counsel publicly opposed the Harris County misdemeanor bail-reform settlement, saying it 'caters to criminals'; said a repeat offender 'should have been behind bars.'

2.note: 10-yr felony prosecutor (Montgomery Co. ICAC/child-exploitation); advocates proportional punishment -- repeat offender 'should have been behind bars.'
Voted AGAINST the Apr 8 2026 ordinance limiting HPD-ICE coordination, joining the 5-member dissent warning it would make officers 'afraid to do their jobs.' Consistent anti-sanctuary.

sallie-alcorn.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. Seat verified: At-Large Position 5, incumbent since 2020, re-elected Nov 2023, second/final term to Jan 2028 (term-limited; Wikipedia, Ballotpedia, houstontx.gov). Chairs the Budget & Fiscal Affairs Committee. marriage q4 FALSE: endorsed by the Houston LGBTQ+ Political Caucus (2019) -- single-issue endorsement is hard evidence she aligns with, not opposes, LGBTQ+ promotion. public_justice q0 TRUE: campaign platform affirmatively pro-police -- 'fully supported all HPD budgets and expenditures to increase police protection,' backs raises/cadets/overtime/gunshot-detection, 'crime stats need to come down.' Economic left null: she voted to RAISE the property-tax rate (against the flat rate, in the 3-vote minority), citing the need for revenue -- a pro-tax-increase posture, not a balanced-budget-amendment position. Immigration left null: initially supported the Apr 2026 ICE-limiting ordinance, then voted (13-4) for the amended rollback to preserve ~$114M in state grants ('protecting Fourth Amendment rights while also protecting our financial future') -- a funding-coerced flip, genuinely mixed. No evidence on life, trans policy, schools, Christian liberty, election, self-defense.

4.note: Endorsed by the Houston LGBTQ+ Political Caucus (2019) -- single-issue endorsement is hard evidence she aligns with LGBTQ+ advocacy, not opposing its promotion.

0.note: Campaign platform affirmatively pro-police: 'fully supported all HPD budgets and expenditures to increase police protection,' backs officer raises, cadet classes, overtime, gunshot detection; 'crime stats need to come down.'
